title: Rich British Beurre-Noisette Gratin
description: A dense and comforting British dish featuring beurre-noisette and double-rise bread, perfect for a hangover cure or as a breakfast treat. This recipe is Buddhist temple-friendly and easy to make.
instructions: In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the garlic and thyme, and cook until fragrant.
In a separate saucepan, warm the beurre-noisette over low heat until melted and fragrant.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the bread cubes, melted butter mixture, and warm beurre-noisette, tossing to coat evenly.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13-inch baking dish, and season with salt and pepper.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the gratin is set.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly before serving.
